name: Three.js (WebGL 3D Library)

performance.notes: WebGL GPU rendering; 60fps for moderate scenes; performance depends on geometry complexity and GPU

agent_readiness.notes: Most popular WebGL 3D library. Scene graph: Mesh, Geometry, Material, Light, Camera, Renderer. Loaders for GLTF, OBJ, FBX, STL. PBR materials (MeshStandardMaterial). Shadow maps, post-processing (bloom, depth of field). Physics via cannon-es or ammo.js integration. React-three-fiber for React integration. Used for: product visualization, 3D games, data visualization, interactive art, scientific visualization. Large examples/ecosystem.

gotchas: Version number is misleading (0.163.x but very mature)
No built-in physics engine (use cannon-es or Rapier integration)
Large bundle size if not tree-shaken
avoid_when: Complex physics simulations (use Unity/Unreal or dedicated engines)
summary: Three.js is the most popular WebGL 3D library for the web. Scene graph with meshes, lights, cameras, and materials. GLTF/OBJ/FBX loaders, PBR materials, shadows, post-processing. React-three-fiber for React integration. Large example ecosystem. Used for product visualization, 3D data viz, games, and interactive art. r3f (React Three Fiber) makes it much easier to use in modern React apps.
